diagram.title: Create Diagram Smoke Flow
diagram.purpose: Exercise clusters, walkthroughs, self-loops, descriptions, and editable export generation.
takeaways: Requests move from actor to service before durable state changes.
The queue and worker path proves asynchronous handoff rendering.
Operational artifacts stay visible for follow-up work.

nodes.label: Requester
nodes.description: Person asking the agent to create a reliable architecture diagram.

nodes.label: HTML Builder
nodes.description: Builds validated HTML from a structured diagram payload.

nodes.label: Shared Template
nodes.description: Canonical renderer that stays unchanged outside data blocks.

nodes.label: HTML Validator
nodes.description: Checks diagram data, metadata, positions, edges, and template drift.

nodes.label: Excalidraw Exporter
nodes.description: Creates an editable Excalidraw file after HTML validation passes.

nodes.label: Editable Artifact
nodes.description: Native Excalidraw document with labels and descriptions.

nodes.label: Notes {JSON}
nodes.description: Literal brace text proves parser strings remain safe.

nodes.label: Ops Review
nodes.description: Review moment that checks generated files before sharing.

nodes.label: Runbook
nodes.description: Operational notes for future agents changing the diagram.

walkthrough.title: Build HTML
walkthrough.description: Requester data enters the builder and is placed into the shared template.

walkthrough.title: Validate and export
walkthrough.description: The validator gates Excalidraw generation and catches data problems first.

walkthrough.title: Review notes
walkthrough.description: Operational notes and review artifacts remain visible for future changes.
